arrive.speaker: narrator
arrive.text: The stones are older than any language you were ever taught. They have been here long enough that the moss on them has its own history. Up close, the center stone is warmer than the air around it. Something has been here recently.

find-book.speaker: narrator
find-book.text: A small leather-bound book lies half-buried in the moss at the base of the stone. The cover is damp and the stitching has begun to loosen, but when you lift it you can feel the weight of a life inside it. The hand is precise. Elven, precise, and afraid.

read-entry.speaker: narrator
read-entry.text: The last entry, written three days ago: "Found the sealing-mark. It is NOT a warding — it is an anchor. Whatever is held in place here, the holding costs. It takes. I must tell Brenna. But the token grows warm when I speak her name. I will not speak it again until I am home."

pages-torn.speaker: narrator
pages-torn.text: The two pages after that entry have been torn out. Not neatly. You turn the book in your hands and the torn edges come away with a thin line of dried blood along one.

token-pulse.speaker: narrator
token-pulse.text: In your pocket, the iron token Brenna gave you pulses once — faint, warm, almost a heartbeat — and is still. Whatever brought Veyrin to this place, it knows that you are here now too.

deeper-hint.speaker: narrator
deeper-hint.text: Beneath the stone, far beneath, you feel a faint tremor — not in the earth, but in the air itself. The cairn is not the bottom. It is a lid. And something below it is patient, and old, and aware that the lid has been touched.

close.speaker: narrator
close.text: You tuck the book into your pack. The woods behind you, suddenly, are silent.
